title: More than touch: understanding how people use skin as an input surface for mobile computing
summary: This paper explores on-skin interaction and treats the body itself as an input surface for mobile computing. It shows that different skin locations afford different gestures and meanings, and that physical comfort or discomfort can be deliberately used as part of interaction design. It is valuable because it frames touch as bodily, anatomical, and affective rather than neutral.

related.reason: Both resist reducing touch to purely technical sensing.
Both broaden touch beyond neutral input.
Both are HCI-oriented interaction papers using touch operationally.

notes: This paper strongly suggests that skin is not just a surface but a differentiated interface with expressive and functional variation. A very interesting point is that uncomfortable gestures can be reserved for irreversible or important actions.
quotes: Touch is redefined here as body-as-interface.
